title: 12 Troubleshooting and Best Practices for Client Centre Users
body_text:
12	Troubleshooting and Best Practices for Client Centre Users
12.1	Overview of Troubleshooting and Best Practices for Client Centre Users
If a user has trouble logging in to the Client Centre, take note of where they encounter the error message, and refer to the table below for next steps:
Description of Problem
Additional Information
User receives an error message after entering Client Centre username and password.
See Section 12.2, Troubleshooting Client Centre Login Problems.
User receives an error or a blank page when accessing an application on the Client Centre.
See Section 12.3, Troubleshooting Client Centre Application Access Problems.
User receives an error when accessing a Client Centre application via a browser bookmark.
See Section 12.4, Best Practices for Accessing Client Centre Applications and Adding Application to Favourites.
User is having trouble logging in with a 2-step security token.
Refer to the 2-Step Security Part.
12.2	Troubleshooting Client Centre Login Problems
If a user has trouble logging in to the Client Centre:
New User
â Wait at least 45 minutes from the time the user is created to the time the user attempts to log in. It can take up to 45 minutes for the back-end systems to synchronize.
Existing User
â
Try resetting the userâs password. See Section 6.6, Resetting a Userâs Password, for procedures.
Note:	Users are locked out for 30 minutes after seven consecutive unsuccessful password login attempts, even if the login attempts are made over several days.
12.3	Troubleshooting Client Centre Application Access Problems
Login Issues Caused by Browser Cookies
A browser cookie is a file saved by a browser and used to identify the individual accessing a websiteâs server. Problems with browser cookies can prevent a user from logging in to a website or accessing an application. If a blank screen or an error message displays when a user attempts to access a Client Centre application, it may be caused by browser cookies. Instruct the user to access the application in a private/incognito browser, as they run without stored data such as cookies and history.
Login Issues Caused by Sharing a Computer with Another User
If the user shares a computer with another user, itâs possible that the other user didnât log out properly. Instruct the user to:
Select
Logout
to log out of the application (if a logout option is available for the application). Wait until the application states that you have successfully logged out. On the upper right section of the Client Centre, select your name, then select
Logout
.
Clear the cookies and/or temporary internet files and close the browser. Refer to your browser provider for instructions.
Restart the browser, log in to the Client Centre, then access the application.
12.4	Best Practices for Accessing Client Centre Applications and Adding Application to Favourites
Best Practices for Accessing Applications
Follow the access procedures described in an applicationâs documentation. We donât recommend bookmarking an application. Users should add commonly used applications to their Client Centre favourites instead.
Note: 	If users bookmark applications, they must ensure the bookmark is added from the application landing page, not in the middle of an application session.
Adding Client Centre Applications to Favourites
Add applications to the âMy Favouritesâ section in the Client Centre to streamline application access.
Add a Client Centre application to favourites as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	Youâll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itâs your first login to the Client Centre, youâve deleted your cookies, or youâre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
Manage favourites
. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select âManage Favouritesâ
Select the check box next to the appropriate application(s), then select
Update Favourites
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Add an Application to Favourites
The next time you access the Client Centreâs Applications page, your favourite application(s) will display in the âMy Favouritesâ section. Select the application link to directly access the application. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: View your Favourite Applications
END
